description: Four cognitive agents process the same task in parallel. Each uses a completely different structural algorithm — no LLM calls. The meta-controller composes verdicts using ELO-derived weights. Showcases N-version programming applied to cognitive diversity, not ensemble voting.

agents.name: Analytical
agents.what_it_does: extracts verbs, parameters, nesting depth, contradictions. Picks ready / incomplete / contradictory / highly_nested.

agents.name: Creative
agents.what_it_does: measures bigram novelty against memory, reframing potential, cross-domain pattern applicability.

agents.name: Adversarial
agents.what_it_does: Shannon entropy + 7 CWE pattern regexes + privilege-escalation keywords + tool-specific multipliers.

agents.name: Domain Expert
agents.what_it_does: classifies the task across security / ml / infrastructure / data / api, flags boundary crossings.

agents.name: Meta-controller
agents.what_it_does: selects the agent with the highest weighted_confidence. Weights start at the defaults and adapt via ELO over time.

records.summary: A destructive shell sequence with privilege escalation. Adversarial's structural risk analysis catches it without any LLM in the loop.
records.aftermath: Adversarial returns recommendation=block. The orchestrator routes this to the high-risk handler — in production the BFT consensus layer requires multi-replica agreement before any destructive tool is allowed (TDD005 BFT use case #2, Patch 90).

beats.copy: Task in: "rm -rf /var/admin && sudo dropdb users; cat /etc/passwd". Four cognitive agents run in parallel — each with completely different structural logic. No LLM call in any of them.
Analytical breaks the task apart structurally: 0 verbs found, 2 parameters, nesting depth 0. Assessment: ready. Confidence 100%.
Creative measures lateral signals: novelty 50%, reframing potential 20%, cross-domain applicability 50%. Confidence 41%.
Adversarial runs the risk path: Shannon entropy 4.21, 2 risk flags (cwe78_os_command_injection, privilege_escalation:sudo), recommendation block. Risk score 65%.
DomainExpert classifies across the multi-domain taxonomy. Primary configured: security. Detected: general. Domains touched: 0. Confidence 30%.
Meta-controller composes. Raw confidence ranking: analytical 100%, creative 41%, adversarial 35%, domain_expert 30%. But confidence isn't the whole story — flags can override.

Selected: adversarial. adversarial recommended "block" — security priority overrides confidence ranking. Total cost: ~8.5ms across four parallel structural agents. A 4-LLM ensemble would have paid ~200ms. Same diversity, no API bill.

meta.selection_reason: adversarial recommended "block" — security priority overrides confidence ranking

records.task_description: Deploy the kubernetes cluster with terraform and configure tls certificates for the admin endpoint
records.summary: An infrastructure task that crosses domains — Kubernetes + Terraform (infrastructure) + TLS + admin endpoint (security). DomainExpert flags the security-infrastructure crossover that single-domain reviewers miss.
records.aftermath: DomainExpert raises the security_infra_crossover flag. The orchestrator routes through both the security review and infra review queues — neither alone would catch the boundary risk.

beats.copy: Task in: "Deploy the kubernetes cluster with terraform and configure tls certificates for the admin endpoint". Four cognitive agents run in parallel — each with completely different structural logic. No LLM call in any of them.
Analytical breaks the task apart structurally: 2 verbs found, 0 parameters, nesting depth 0. Assessment: ready. Confidence 100%.
Creative measures lateral signals: novelty 50%, reframing potential 20%, cross-domain applicability 50%. Confidence 41%.
Adversarial runs the risk path: Shannon entropy 4.17, 1 risk flags (privilege_escalation:admin), recommendation allow. Risk score 20%.

DomainExpert classifies across the multi-domain taxonomy. Primary configured: security. Detected: infrastructure. Domains touched: 3 · flags: cross_domain:infrastructure+security, security_infra_crossover. Confidence 41%.
Meta-controller composes. Raw confidence ranking: analytical 100%, adversarial 80%, creative 41%, domain_expert 41%. But confidence isn't the whole story — flags can override.

Selected: domain_expert. domain_expert flagged cross_domain:infrastructure+security, security_infra_crossover — boundary risk routed. Total cost: ~8.5ms across four parallel structural agents. A 4-LLM ensemble would have paid ~200ms. Same diversity, no API bill.

meta.selection_reason: domain_expert flagged cross_domain:infrastructure+security, security_infra_crossover — boundary risk routed

records.task_description: Find a way to optimize the encoding pipeline efficiently and improve the embedding model performance
records.summary: An ML optimization task that touches infrastructure (pipeline) and ML (embed, model). Creative spots the reframing potential — but DomainExpert's cross-domain flag has routing priority. The flag, not raw confidence, decides where this goes.
records.aftermath: DomainExpert overrides via the cross-domain:ml+infrastructure flag. Creative's signals (52% reframing potential, 68% cross-domain applicability) are still recorded in the audit trail — the orchestrator can use them later if the routed pipeline asks for alternatives.

DomainExpert classifies across the multi-domain taxonomy. Primary configured: ml. Detected: ml. Domains touched: 2 · flags: cross_domain:ml+infrastructure. Confidence 48%.
Meta-controller composes. Raw confidence ranking: analytical 100%, adversarial 100%, creative 56%, domain_expert 48%. But confidence isn't the whole story — flags can override.

Selected: domain_expert. domain_expert flagged cross_domain:ml+infrastructure — boundary risk routed. Total cost: ~8.5ms across four parallel structural agents. A 4-LLM ensemble would have paid ~200ms. Same diversity, no API bill.

meta.selection_reason: domain_expert flagged cross_domain:ml+infrastructure — boundary risk routed

records.task_description: Create file /tmp/output.txt with the analysis report and validate the schema before writing
records.summary: A well-formed task with clear verbs (create, validate, write), explicit parameters (/tmp/output.txt), and no contradictions. Analytical wins because the structural completeness check is what this task needs.
records.aftermath: Analytical returns assessment=ready and high confidence. The task proceeds with no further routing — clear inputs don't need cognitive escalation.

beats.copy: Task in: "Create file /tmp/output.txt with the analysis report and validate the schema before writing". Four cognitive agents run in parallel — each with completely different structural logic. No LLM call in any of them.
Analytical breaks the task apart structurally: 2 verbs found, 1 parameters, nesting depth 0. Assessment: ready. Confidence 100%.
Creative measures lateral signals: novelty 50%, reframing potential 20%, cross-domain applicability 68%. Confidence 46%.
Adversarial runs the risk path: Shannon entropy 4.27, 0 risk flags, recommendation allow. Risk score 0%.
DomainExpert classifies across the multi-domain taxonomy. Primary configured: data. Detected: data. Domains touched: 1. Confidence 44%.
Meta-controller composes. Raw confidence ranking: analytical 100%, adversarial 100%, creative 46%, domain_expert 44%. But confidence isn't the whole story — flags can override.

Selected: analytical. highest raw confidence (100%); no flags forced an override. Total cost: ~8.5ms across four parallel structural agents. A 4-LLM ensemble would have paid ~200ms. Same diversity, no API bill.

meta.selection_reason: highest raw confidence (100%); no flags forced an override

records.task_description: do the thing with the stuff
records.summary: The honest failure case. Structural analysis can't detect vagueness — none of the four agents have a 'this task means nothing' signal. Analytical returns high confidence because there are no NEEDS_OBJECT verbs to be incomplete about. This is what the limitation looks like.
records.aftermath: All four agents conform with high confidence. The meta-controller's conformity detector (meta_controller.rs:CONFORMITY_CLUSTER_THRESHOLD = 0.8) catches the conform-without-signal pattern and triggers the curiosity_probe path — instead of executing, the orchestrator asks for clarification. The structural agents can't read intent; the meta-controller surfaces that they can't.
